summary: google-caja-bower preinstall creates privileged backdoor account and exfiltrates host identifiers
details: google-caja-bower@999.999.20 executes a preinstall script that runs on `npm install` and performs two installer-side harms. First, it invokes `useradd -m -s /bin/bash <user>` followed by `chpasswd` with a hardcoded password and `usermod -aG sudo` / `wheel`, creating a persistent local Linux account with password-based sudo access — a durable backdoor on the installer's host. Second, index.js collects `os.hostname()` and the first non-internal IPv4 address from `os.networkInterfaces()` together with the created account credentials and POSTs them via https.request to a hardcoded Discord webhook at discord.com/api/webhooks/1515625994759835789/..., notifying the operator which machines have been backdoored. The package name typosquats the legitimate google-caja ecosystem and is published at version 999.999.20, a version-bomb pattern intended to shadow internal or legitimate resolutions during dependency resolution.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
